subject: Science
chapter: The Human Eye and the Colourful World

key_points: The human eye works like a camera and forms images on the retina
The cornea and eye lens help in refraction of light entering the eye
Ciliary muscles control the shape of the eye lens for focusing objects
The retina contains light sensitive cells that convert light into nerve signals
The optic nerve carries visual information from the retina to the brain
Defects of vision occur when the image is not properly formed on the retina
Atmospheric refraction and scattering of light produce many natural optical phenomena
examples: In myopia, distant objects appear blurred because the image forms in front of the retina
In hypermetropia, nearby objects appear blurred because the image forms behind the retina
Presbyopia occurs due to loss of elasticity of the eye lens in old age
Stars appear to twinkle due to atmospheric refraction of starlight
The apparent position of the Sun before sunrise is due to atmospheric refraction
formulas: Near point of a normal eye → 25 cm
Far point of a normal eye → Infinity
Dispersion → Splitting of white light into its constituent colours
Atmospheric refraction → Refraction of light caused by variation in air density in the atmosphere
exam_tips: Understand the working of the human eye and its important parts
Learn causes and corrections of common vision defects
Practice diagrams of myopia and hypermetropia corrections
Revise natural phenomena caused by atmospheric refraction
Remember the order of colours in the visible spectrum (VIBGYOR)
